NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD’s, and all its fullness, The world and those who dwell therein.
NKJV.2: For He has founded it upon the seas, And established it upon the waters.
NKJV.3: Who may ascend into the hill of the LORD? Or who may stand in His holy place?
NKJV.4: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, Who has not lifted up his soul to an idol, Nor sworn deceitfully.
NKJV.5: He shall receive blessing from the LORD, And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
NKJV.6: This is Jacob, the generation of those who seek Him, Who seek Your face. Selah
NKJV.7: Lift up your heads, O you gates! And be lifted up, you everlasting doors!  And the King of glory shall come in.
NKJV.8: Who is this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, The LORD mighty in battle.
NKJV.9: Lift up your heads, O you gates! Lift up, you everlasting doors! And the King of glory shall come in.
NKJV.10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, He is the King of glory. Selah

KJV.5: He shall receive<S>5375</S> the blessing<S>1293</S> from the LORD<S>3068</S>, and righteousness<S>6666</S> from the God<S>430</S> of his salvation<S>3468</S>.
KJV.6: This is the generation<S>1755</S> of them that seek<S>1875</S> him, that seek<S>1245</S> thy face<S>6440</S>, O Jacob<S>3290</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>O Jacob: or, O God of Jacob</sup>
KJV.7: Lift up<S>5375</S> your heads<S>7218</S>, O ye gates<S>8179</S>; and be ye lift up<S>5375</S>, ye everlasting<S>5769</S> doors<S>6607</S>; and the King<S>4428</S> of glory<S>3519</S> shall come in<S>935</S>.

ESV.1: The earth is the LORD's and the fullness thereof,  the world and those who dwell therein,
ESV.2: for he has founded it upon the seas and established it upon the rivers.
ESV.3: Who shall ascend the hill of the LORD? And who shall stand in his holy place?
ESV.4: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, who does not lift up his soul to what is false and does not swear deceitfully.
ESV.5: He will receive blessing from the LORD and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
ESV.6: Such is the generation of those who seek him, who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Selah
ESV.7: Lift up your heads, O gates! And be lifted up, O ancient doors, that the King of glory may come in.
ESV.8: Who is this King of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty, the LORD, mighty in battle!
ESV.9: Lift up your heads, O gates! And lift them up, O ancient doors, that the King of glory may come in.
ESV.10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, he is the King of glory! Selah

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD'S, and all it contains, The world, and those who dwell in it.
NASB.2: For He has founded it upon the seas And established it upon the rivers.
NASB.3: Who may ascend into the hill of the LORD? And who may stand in His holy place?
NASB.4: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, Who has not lifted up his soul to falsehood And has not sworn deceitfully.
NASB.5: He shall receive a blessing from the LORD And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
NASB.6: This is the generation of those who seek Him, Who seek Your face-- [even] Jacob. Selah.
NASB.7: Lift up your heads, O gates, And be lifted up, O ancient doors, That the King of glory may come in!
NASB.8: Who is the King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, The LORD mighty in battle.
NASB.9: Lift up your heads, O gates, And lift [them] up, O ancient doors, That the King of glory may come in!
NASB.10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, He is the King of glory. Selah.

NLT.1:
The earth is the LORD’s, and everything in it.
The world and all its people belong to him.
NLT.2:
For he laid the earth’s foundation on the seas
and built it on the ocean depths.
NLT.3:
Who may climb the mountain of the LORD?
Who may stand in his holy place?
NLT.4:
Only those whose hands and hearts are pure,
who do not worship idols
and never tell lies.
NLT.5:
They will receive the LORD’s blessing
and have a right relationship with God their savior.
NLT.6:
Such people may seek you
and worship in your presence, O God of Jacob. Interlude
NLT.7:
Open up, ancient gates!
Open up, ancient doors,
and let the King of glory enter.
NLT.8:
Who is the King of glory?
The LORD, strong and mighty;
the LORD, invincible in battle.
NLT.9:
Open up, ancient gates!
Open up, ancient doors,
and let the King of glory enter.
NLT.10:
Who is the King of glory?
The LORD of Heaven’s Armies —
he is the King of glory. Interlude

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. The earth is Yahweh’s, with its fullness; the world, and those who dwell therein.
WEB.2: For he has founded it on the seas, and established it on the floods.
WEB.3: Who may ascend to Yahweh’s hill? Who may stand in his holy place?
WEB.4: He who has clean hands and a pure heart; who has not lifted up his soul to falsehood, and has not sworn deceitfully.
WEB.5: He shall receive a blessing from Yahweh, righteousness from the God of his salvation.
WEB.6: This is the generation of those who seek Him, who seek your face — even Jacob. Selah.
WEB.7: Lift up your heads, you gates! Be lifted up, you everlasting doors, and the King of glory will come in.
WEB.8: Who is the King of glory? Yahweh strong and mighty, Yahweh mighty in battle.
WEB.9: Lift up your heads, you gates; yes, lift them up, you everlasting doors, and the King of glory will come in.
WEB.10: Who is this King of glory? Yahweh of Armies is the King of glory! Selah.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David. [1] The earth and everything in it,
the world and its inhabitants,
belong to the LORD; ⓖ
CSB17.2:
for he laid its foundation on the seas
and established it on the rivers. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
Who may ascend the mountain of the LORD?
Who may stand in his holy place? ⓘ
CSB17.4:
The one who has clean hands and a pure heart, ⓙ
who has not appealed to [1] what is false, ⓚ
and who has not sworn deceitfully.
CSB17.5:
He will receive blessing from the LORD, ⓛ
and righteousness [2] from the God of his salvation. ⓜ
CSB17.6:
Such is the generation of those who inquire of him,
who seek the face of the God of Jacob. [3] ⓝ Selah
CSB17.7:
Lift up your heads, you gates!
Rise up, ancient doors!
Then the King of glory will come in. ⓐ
CSB17.8:
Who is this King of glory?
The LORD, strong and mighty,
the LORD, mighty in battle. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
Lift up your heads, you gates!
Rise up, ancient doors!
Then the King of glory will come in.
CSB17.10:
Who is he, this King of glory?
The LORD of Armies,
he is the King of glory. ⓒ Selah

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. The earth is the Lord's, and the fullness of it,
The world, and those who dwell in it.
AMP.2:
For He has founded it upon the seas
And established it upon the streams and the rivers.
AMP.3:
Who may ascend onto the mountain of the LORD?
And who may stand in His holy place?
AMP.4:
He who has clean hands and a pure heart,
Who has not lifted up his soul to what is false,
Nor has sworn [oaths] deceitfully.
AMP.5:
He shall receive a blessing from the LORD,
And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
AMP.6:
This is the generation (description) of those who diligently seek Him and require Him as their greatest need,
Who seek Your face, even [as did] Jacob. Selah.
AMP.7:
Lift up your heads, O gates,
And be lifted up, ancient doors,
That the King of glory may come in.
AMP.8:
Who is the King of glory?
The LORD strong and mighty,
The LORD mighty in battle.
AMP.9:
Lift up your heads, O gates,
And lift them up, ancient doors,
That the King of glory may come in.
AMP.10:
Who is [He then] this King of glory?
The LORD of hosts,
He is the King of glory [who rules over all creation with His heavenly armies]. Selah.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God claims Earth and everything in it,
God claims World and all who live on it.
MSG.2:
He built it on Ocean foundations,
laid it out on River girders.
MSG.3:
Who can climb Mount God?
Who can scale the holy north-face?
MSG.4:
Only the clean-handed,
only the pure-hearted;
Men who won't cheat,
women who won't seduce.
MSG.5:
God is at their side;
with God's help they make it.
MSG.6:
This, Jacob, is what happens
to God-seekers, God-questers.
MSG.7:
Wake up, you sleepyhead city!
Wake up, you sleepyhead people!
King-Glory is ready to enter.
MSG.8:
Who is this King-Glory?
God, armed
and battle-ready.
MSG.9:
Wake up, you sleepyhead city!
Wake up, you sleepyhead people!
King-Glory is ready to enter.
MSG.10:
Who is this King-Glory?
God of the angel armies:
he is King-Glory.

NIV.1:
Psalm 24
Of David. A psalm.
The earth is the Lord’s, and everything in it,
the world, and all who live in it;
NIV.2:
for he founded it upon the seas
and established it upon the waters.
NIV.3:
Who may ascend the hill of the Lord?
Who may stand in his holy place?
NIV.4:
He who has clean hands and a pure heart,
who does not lift up his soul to an idol
or swear by what is false.
NIV.5:
He will receive blessing from the Lord
and vindication from God his Savior.
NIV.6:
Such is the generation of those who seek him,
who seek your face, O God of Jacob. Selah
NIV.7:
Lift up your heads, O you gates;
be lifted up, you ancient doors,
that the King of glory may come in.
NIV.8:
Who is this King of glory?
The Lord strong and mighty,
the Lord mighty in battle.
NIV.9:
Lift up your heads, O you gates;
lift them up, you ancient doors,
that the King of glory may come in.
NIV.10:
Who is he, this King of glory?
The Lord Almighty—
he is the King of glory. Selah

NRSVCE.1:
The earth is the LORD’s and all that is in it,
the world, and those who live in it;
NRSVCE.2:
for he has founded it on the seas,
and established it on the rivers.
NRSVCE.3:
Who shall ascend the hill of the LORD?
And who shall stand in his holy place?
NRSVCE.4:
Those who have clean hands and pure hearts,
who do not lift up their souls to what is false,
and do not swear deceitfully.
NRSVCE.5:
They will receive blessing from the LORD,
and vindication from the God of their salvation.
NRSVCE.6:
Such is the company of those who seek him,
who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Selah
NRSVCE.7:
Lift up your heads, O gates!
and be lifted up, O ancient doors!
that the King of glory may come in.
NRSVCE.8:
Who is the King of glory?
The LORD, strong and mighty,
the LORD, mighty in battle.
NRSVCE.9:
Lift up your heads, O gates!
and be lifted up, O ancient doors!
that the King of glory may come in.
NRSVCE.10:
Who is this King of glory?
The LORD of hosts,
he is the King of glory. Selah

NET.1: The LORD owns the earth and all it contains, the world and all who live in it.
NET.2: For he set its foundation upon the seas, and established it upon the ocean currents.
NET.3: Who is allowed to ascend the mountain of the LORD? Who may go up to his holy dwelling place?
NET.4: The one whose deeds are blameless and whose motives are pure, who does not lie, or make promises with no intention of keeping them.
NET.5: Such godly people are rewarded by the LORD, and vindicated by the God who delivers them.
NET.6: Such purity characterizes the people who seek his favor, Jacob’s descendants, who pray to him. (Selah)
NET.7: Look up, you gates! Rise up, you eternal doors! Then the majestic king will enter!
NET.8: Who is this majestic king? The LORD who is strong and mighty! The LORD who is mighty in battle!
NET.9: Look up, you gates! Rise up, you eternal doors! Then the majestic king will enter!
NET.10: Who is this majestic king? The LORD who commands armies! He is the majestic king! (Selah)
